analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: BS implied vol from voucher mid vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; vega at implied sigma; vega-weighted mean IV time series vs extract 1-tick return. Spread (ask1−bid1) by voucher. TTE: DTE_open=8−csv_day; DTE_eff=DTE_open−(timestamp//100)/10000; T=DTE_eff/365 per round3description.txt + combined_analysis winding (same as plot_iv_smile_round3). Backtest merged timestamps: csv_day = timestamp//1e6, local_ts = timestamp%1e6.
used_to_decide: Negative correlation (~−0.38) between change in vega-weighted IV and concurrent extract return suggests tilting quotes with extract momentum is directionally consistent with surface co-movement. Mean spread rises for low strikes (VEV_4000 ~21 vs VEV_6500 ~1), so prefer tight inside quotes on liquid strikes and avoid naive wide synthetic spreads. v0’s directional extract leg was abandoned after backtest showed it dominated losses; v2+ use integer shift from inside quotes so OTM strikes still quote.

analysis_description: Greek/microstructure ranking across Round 3 tapes: per-strike mean vega from smile model and mean spread; computed vega_per_spread and return correlation with extract. Top efficiency strikes: 5400, 5300, 5500, 5200. Tested strike-filtered variants (v5/v6/v8) and found severe fill degradation under worse-trade matching; all-strike quoting (v4/v9) remained better.
used_to_decide: Despite high vega-per-spread on mid strikes, restricting traded strikes reduced participation and PnL (v5/v6=0). This falsifies the 'aggressive strike filter' extension for current quote style under this backtester. The concept remains valid as all-strike surface tilt (v4/v9) where extract-driven shift still improves over naive variants.

analysis_description: Per-strike BS delta and vega at smile IV on Round-3 tapes; paired with |correlation| of (voucher mid - extract mid) with extract mid (moneyness co-movement). Output tabulates mean |delta| and mean vega by voucher. Method: same TTE winding as traders; r=0.
used_to_decide: Delta magnitude is huge on deep ITM low strikes (informational) while vega concentrates near 5000-5400. v10 blended |delta| into tilt scale to align with 'track underlying' without hard strike lists; v12 added spread-based dampening after seeing wide books on deep wings in prior spread tables. v11 (no hydrogel) underperformed v4, so hydrogel MM still adds in this harness.

analysis_description: Regime check: per subsampled timestamp, mean BS-implied vol across all ten VEV mids (r=0, same TTE winding); correlate extract 1-tick return with forward first-difference of that mean IV along the tape within each day. Documents joint IV extract dynamics without overfitting.
used_to_decide: Correlation ~-0.17 between extract return and step change in mean IV suggests modest co-movement; reinforces keeping tilt bounded (spread dampening in v12/v14) rather than large unconstrained shifts. v14 tightened spread multipliers and improved backtest total vs v12.

analysis_description: Smile / skew proxy: BS implied vol (r=0) from mid at wing strikes (4000+6500) minus mean of mid at 5200+5300, subsampled across Round-3 days; TTE as traders. Positive spread indicates wings carry higher mkt IV than the nearby-money pair on average.
used_to_decide: Mean (wing) IV - (mid) IV ~+0.37, consistent with a smile. Extra dampening on very wide per-voucher books (v17 third tier) nudged PnL up vs v14; further lowering global TILT_AMP (v18) only matched v16, so signal amplitude 4.0 remains a local optimum next to v14.

analysis_description: Spread-bucket microstructure pass on Round-3 VEV books: grouped observations by spread buckets <=2, 3-5, 6-8, >=9 and computed mean/95th percentile of absolute mid-step change as a volatility proxy. Kept IV/Greek thread via existing smile/IV metrics and used this to calibrate tilt dampening by spread.
used_to_decide: Wider spreads show higher abs mid-step volatility (mean abs step rises from ~0.11 at <=2 to ~0.87 at >=9), supporting stronger dampening for wide books. v19-v21 tested this directly; v20 (0.85/0.66/0.54, VEV_SIZE=18) delivered new best 16,246.

analysis_description: Greek vs spread: pooled all 10 VEVs; for each (day,timestamp,product) row compute smile-IV vega at (S_t,T,K) and bucket by bid-ask width. (Mean vega in the wide bucket is low largely because that bucket over-represents deep-ITM/OTM names with structurally small vega, not a claim that a single option's vega rises when its spread widens.)
used_to_decide: v22 slightly tightened the spread-dampening multipliers again and set a new backtest high (16,260) vs v20. Earlier third-tier threshold shift (v23) and VEV size trim (v24) did not beat v22.

analysis_description: Operational robustness + fills evidence pass: saved full backtest logs for representative best (v22) and nearby control (v24), then parsed day/product PnL into a compact fills summary JSON. Also confirms multi-day processing under round=3 worse-fills setup.
used_to_decide: v22 outperforms v24 by +14, driven by VEV_4000 edge (+6 day0, +2 day1, +6 day2) with identical hydrogel path; supports keeping v22 spread multipliers as current best local setting.

analysis_description: Family-10 weighting robustness check for smile_quadratic_logm_wls: per sampled timestamp fit quadratic IV(log-moneyness) under three schemes (unweighted, inverse-spread WLS, spread+sqrt(vega) hybrid WLS). Compared RMSE, weighted RMSE, and coefficient stability via repeated half-sample refits.
used_to_decide: On tape-level fitting, inverse-spread and hybrid improve weighted RMSE but worsen coefficient stability vs unweighted in this setup. In live backtests, both WLS variants underperform current best v22 (16,260): v25=15,508, v26=15,700. Therefore keep v22 as best for now and document this family-10 variation as explored and non-improving on current architecture.

analysis_description: Underlying propagation deep-dive by contract: dv regressed on contemporaneous/lagged extract move (du, du_l1, du_l2), up/down asymmetry, spread response after top-decile extract shocks, and mean BS delta from market-implied IV. Built contract-level response maps (beta_0, beta_l1, spread-shock sign) and wired them into strike-specific tilt multipliers.
used_to_decide: Low strikes (4000-5100) show strongest immediate beta_0, while 5200+ have weaker response and in some cases positive spread widening after shocks. v27/v28 used these maps to dampen tilt where shock-spread risk is higher and include mild lag where beta_l1 non-trivial. Results did not beat v22 (16260), so v22 remains best local setting.

analysis_description: No new offline tape run; two small v22 ablations: (1) nudge TILT_AMP+W_Z and slightly relax v22 spread-damp multipliers to sit between v20 and v22 on the Pareto border; (2) more mean-reversion emphasis via lower TILT_AMP and higher W_Z. Both backtested round 3 with --match-trades worse and logged fills to JSON.
used_to_decide: v30 (3.9 amp, 0.57 W_Z) reached 16,248 vs v22 16,260, improving on v21-style higher W_Z alone; day-2 hydrogel drag slightly smaller (-1896 vs -1902 on comparable paths). v29 (0.85/0.65/0.53 mults) fell to 16,232, confirming v22's tighter 0.84/0.64/0.52 ladder. Best candidate remains trader_v22.py.

analysis_description:
Tight 5200/5300 book regime on tape (replicated round3work/vouchers_final_strategy story): for each (day, ts) with aligned VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, VEV_5200, VEV_5300, define tight = (s_5200<=2 and s_5300<=2). P(tight) ~0.10-0.17 days 0-2; spread levels correlate with extract mid (e.g. corr(s5200, m_ext) 0.37-0.47). Mean |extract mid step| is not meaningfully lower on tight vs not-tight rows, so the gate is a microstructure/liquidity state flag rather than a low-vol filter for |du|.

After merging origin/main, Prosperity4Data has prices_round_3_day_3.csv, so `prosperity4bt <trader> 3` backtests an extra day; for apples-to-apples with the ~16.26k (days 0-2) target, we now pass explicit 3-0 3-1 3-2. Re-baselined v22 and ran v31/v32 on that slice.
used_to_decide: Implemented v22+v31: when both VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 synthetic bid-ask widths are <=2, scale integer tilt by 1.1 (tighter surface/ATM reference). v32 adds the same v9 inventory skew. On days 0-2 worse-matching, v31 and v32 both total 16,254 vs v22 16,260; the tight-gate slightly hurts day-0 VEV_4000 edge. Keep trader_v22.py as the current best; use explicit 3-0 3-1 3-2 in future reports.

analysis_description: Extract-shock co-movement with the deep-ITM VEV_4000 book (R3 d0-2, aligned per timestamp): adu = |u_mid[t]-u_mid[t-1]|. V4000 |mid step| is much larger in top decile of adu (mean ~1.75) vs below-median adu (mean ~0.73); corr(adu, |dv4000|) ~0.41-0.43, while V4000 level spread s4000 is essentially flat across adu (corr near 0). IV/Greek: tilt still uses static smile-vega scale; the shock is about price-step coupling, not book width. Implication: large single-tick |ret1| is a strong joint-move state; a mild down-scale on tilt only in that window can reduce whipsaw without relying on a spread signal that is not there for V4000 on tape.
used_to_decide: v33: v22 + 0.9x tilt when |ret1|>1.0. v34: v22 + 0.22*clip(ret2) in the tilt (short memory). v33 total 16,273 (beats v22 16,260 and prior best 16,260) on 3-0/1/2 worse. v34 16,213. New best: trader_v33.py.

analysis_description: Reread round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt: tight = BBO (ask1-bid1) on VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 both <= TH=2; forward extract mid (K=20) more favorable on average when both tight (see outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt, day0 Welch t~4.7). Implementation for trader_v35: same TH on live BBO to flag joint_tight; scale integer VEV tilt by 1.06 when on; one-tick-inside VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT when joint_tight; keep v33 shock damp. Replicated STRATEGY spread definition for the gate; voucher tilt path unchanged from prior (synth spread ladder).
used_to_decide: trader_v35.py: v33 + joint BBO tight gate (5200&5300) with +6% VEV tilt and extract MM in gate. R3 d0-2: total 18,232 in both worse and all (identical in this run); VEV+extract only subtotal 10,599 from printed lines. Beats v33 total 16,273. Future: tune gate mult / extract size; optional hydrogel-off variant for 200k/day VEV+U target.
